I have 5 5-year warranty on the battery but theserviceteam says that replacement batteries is not available with tvs company because there is a shortage of batteries, if there is a shortage how company sell new TVs IQube to customers where they get batteries for new scooters. Please don't buy tvs iqube is the worst scooter ever, and the worstserviceby tvs, go buy honda activa instead of this garbage.Almost lost my hard-earned money 150000/ on this garbage. After mailing many times, no proper reply from them.

review_text: How good the TVS iQube EV scooter is a very much premature stage to claim how good it is in the field conditions. But some of the points to be looked can be discussed as follows. Battery - with it's 2.2 kw battery it's more than enough in city ride conditions say for everyday commute. With this power iqube offers a range of75 km/h which is slightly better than Chinese rebadged scooters from other competitors. Motor power of 4.5 kw offers a better acceleration of 0-40kmph in 4.2 seconds which is slightly better too given the other competitors. A kerb weight of 118 kgs offer better dynamic riding conditions. With the low price tag next to Chetak as 1.15 lakhs is literally a competitive priced EV scooter in India than Ather and other vehicles. A charging time of 4-5 hours is enough for a day's ride with average city riding office to home commute. With geo-fencing and other loaded options vehicle looks cooler and slim compared to the others in market. Personally i would say the final performance and acceptability verdict is to be given by the sales numbers alone. That will really show how good the acceptance rate of the vehicle is. Thanks.

review_text: Buying experience - Chennai on road price is 115580/- but they are charging hefty amount on charger cable. Simple charger cable with industrial socket they have priced it as Rs.8260 which should be priced only Rs.2000/- to Rs.3000/-. Tvs has maintained vehicle price equal to competitor but huge margin in chargers and accessories.Ride comfort with pillion is good and it can be used only for fixed distance usage, like only commuting to office and back to home.When 100% charged vehicle pick up is good and top speed achieved under power mode84 km/h, in eco mode top speed is only 52 kmh. But when the power is at less than 70%, eco mode top speed is 44 to46 km/h only. As per my experience under combination eco & power mode 68% and 32% respectively, the range achieved is71.9 kmwith balance 9% charge which shows distance to empty is7kmso approx. total range is78.9 km. The above range is with regenerative option but we could not see how much regenerative power generated in %, app do not provide any dataVery poor app design and bt connectivity issue continues and reports are not generated for a selected period.Unnecessarily head light is permanently "on", even thought there is one drl, horn sound is worst and not good. They should provide ntorq horn which is louder and good.Charging time is huge and it takes 6 hours to charge from 9% to 100%, tvs should come out with replaceable battery at least for the under seat battery so we can buy one spare battery and we can use it as reserve power back up option.The app should provide the details for every trip how much power % consumed and while charging it should provide how much units consumed for every chargeServicingandmaintenanceexperience not able to comment we have wait and watch, tvs should provide improvements as stated above once they fix the issue to all existing customers especially removable battery option.

review_text: The tvs iqube is supposed to be a game changer for tvs in its ev debut but sadly it is let down by step motherly treatment by tvs itself.Buying was problematic as tvs as chosen only 4-5 showrooms in all of bangalore to sell iqube. For some reason, showrooms are not enthusiastic about giving test ride, following up, picking calls etc. All the push needs to come from the customer side till they get vehicle delivery. Looks like tvs itself in not eager about selling iqube in big numbers.The iqube rides very well in city conditions and like a very conventional scooter minus the petrol engine noise and vibrations. No complaints there. No hassles with pillion rider either.Build quality is not great .Plastic feel throughout and not the nice kind as well. Gaps between panels are clearly visible. The key fob is a prime example of giving flashiness the upper hand over quality. Mine simply split into two after a few days.Charging time takes 6-7 hours for 10% to 100% and gives upto75kmriding distance. Good for short distance rides. Charger price is extra, which is plain dumb considering every ev should have a charger in its package. The most cheapest electronic product ships with a charger and tvs chose not to. Clearly the accessory pays more gimmick. Also charging point needs a good earthing presence else vehicle cannot be charged. You will not find this mentioned in showroom.Vehiclemaintenanceis not much, just keep tires inflated and use water to clean and that's all it takes.But the onus is on the owner to keep the vehicle problem-free because iqubeservicecenters are very few and you cannot get it repaired in local garages. You are at the mercy of the tvsservicecenter which can repair it. Tvs having so much local presence already should have at least ensured that iqube can be repaired at anyservicecenter. Again this feels deliberate. My nearestservicecenter for iqube is nearly15 kmaway but there are more than 8 tvsservicecenters on the way which do not deal with iqube. Also spares are not compatible with any of their other products.Another thing is that tvs itself seems perplexed with many of the random things that the scooter does. My vehicle shows up with a "close throttle warning" at starting and tvs has not been able to tell why that comes and what the solution is. The only so called solution is to twist the throttle and leave it and hope the warning goes away. Clearly the sensor calibration is what the issue is but they want customer to change the way they drive to solve their problem. I have heard another user say sometimes the battery indicator shows 0% even if there is enough charge and need to restart the vehicle.Pros:- decent ev for someone whose daily travel is50kmand has charging facilities at home.- very good motor. Zippy, zero noise, responsive.- great handling and suspension and good ground clearance.- not complicated to use at all even with lots of electronic features.- easy to maintain.Cons:- reliability - range anxiety of ev aside, you never know which sensor might get knocked off on the iqube and the vehicle gets bricked (keep towingservicesnumber handy). Tvs should do more customer interaction to know the regular problems faced by users and only then they can solve.- serviceability is poor. Customerservicealmost has no knowledge of the scooter working and their only response is to ask theservicecenter to call you.- takes way to long to charge.- there is no concept of software upgrade. Awful app.There is a lot of work for tvs to do with iqube if it wants to be considered a serious ev player. Otherwise, this model will just be a footnote in the ev revolution.

review_text: We have 2 iQube scooters and are now regretting purchasing these scooters.the motor of one scooter and the battery of both scooters had to be changed after around one year of use.Partsare not available at theservicecenter, and after you deposit the scooter at theservicecenter, they order theparts, which come from Chennai. So, god forbid if you have any problem, your scooter will be with theservicecenter for 20 to 25 days, after which you can collect your vehicle. While thepartswere free of cost since they were in warranty, we were charged labour costs for their replacement. Another problem is that even if you have to get your tyre replaced, you will have to get it done at theservicecenter since the wheels have sensors, so the normal tyre vendors cannot change the tyres, which they would otherwise do for free if it were a normal petrol scooter or car. Theservicecenter charged us around Rs. 500/- for changing one tyre. When we called the customer care toll-free number, they did not provide us with information regarding the labor charges as per company rules. They told us that they have no information regarding the same and only theservicecenter will tell us, In other words the company has given a free hand to dealers to loot the customers because they have no option but to get the job done there only, If you get your tyre punctured you will have to spend 500/- every time if you want to get a proper puncture made. Otherwise, you will have to get the puncture repaired by an outside method, which destroys your tyre.The call center executives who pick up your call are idiots. They will tell you that you will get a call back within the next 48 hrs, which you will never get.We are facing so many problems even when our scooters are in the warranty period. We don't know what will happen after the warranty is over. If you are thinking of buying this scooter, thinking you will save on petrol, you will be looted by theservicecenter much more than what you will save on petrol.

review_text: Biggest problem1 While driving the scooty, its display suddenly turned off. After the display is turned off, the scooty is not starting. I drove the scooty on the middle of the road away from the city, holding it in my hand. For some time, I would drive it while holding it and my son would push it from behind. For some time, my son would drive it and I would keep pushing it from behind. I drove the scooty like this for5 kms, after that I kept it at some acquaintance's place.This problem had come to me a month ago too, even then, with the help of roadside assistant, I took the scooty to the workshop, there also I was told that its software will be updated. But today again it turned off and I had to drive the scooty in the middle of the road by holding it in my hand. The scooty for which I spent one and a half lakh rupees, I have to drive it by holding it in my hand. The scooty I bought in the name of TVS company is so poor that I feel embarrassed in front of my relatives. Even strangers make me feel ashamed when they see me riding an electric scooty while holding it in my .2 Apart from this, the triangle ignition gets turned on while driving this scooty and the vehicle does not run at more than 20-22 speeds. It has to be switched off and started again, only then it starts and this problem occurs several times a day. The company has no solution for it.3 If the scooty falls into a small pit while driving, the rim of its rear wheel gets bent. The rim is made of aluminum which is weaker than iron and because of is a tubeless tire, air comes out of it when it gets bent and when I took it to the company to fix the bend, they asked for an expense of Rs. 2000 to 2200. After getting it repaired once, I drove it for some time and it got bent again. Now how many times should I spend Rs. 2000-2200? If I get it repaired outside, the company voids the warranty.I am not able to understand what to do. Should I go to the consumer forum? Please suggest.

review_text: I purchased this in April 2022 and the sales staff was clueless about the new vehicle launch in May 2022. I asked specifically if there is a new model coming but they had no idea.The scooter is well built and has good features but compared to other ev scooters, it lacks a lot. The most important lacking is its range. The offered range is75 km/charge but the real range in eco mode is approx50km. It's a huge setback considering there are models in other brands which cost the same but offer a better range. I would advise anybody looking to buy this vehicle to first consult with existing owners.

review_text: I recently bought the TVS iQube 2.2 kWh, and my overall experience has been very good. The buying experience was smooth, and the staff explained everything clearly about charging and features. The scooter looks premium and modern, especially in black. Riding is very comfortable, acceleration is smooth, and it is perfect for daily city use. The range is decent for short commutes, and charging is easy at home.In terms of performance, it is silent and hassle-free compared to petrol scooters.Maintenanceis low, and theservicingexperience so far has been satisfactory. Pros include low running costs, a smooth ride, and an eco-friendly nature. Cons are limited top speed and range for longtrips.Maintenancetip: always keep tyre pressure correct, avoid deep discharge, and charge regularly to maintain battery health.

review_text: Their chargers are not repairable other than some wires and pure electricalpartsoutside of the main charger compartment.Also, the wings of the fan fitted within the guard on the main body break without any impact, without external hammering, without any physical damage.If this happens, the fan still runs, and at the same time, if the charger stops working, they claim it's physical damage, and you have to pay for a new charger at about 14k.Just for a small fan wing, you had to go for a new charger purchase, and they call it ECO FRIENDLY, and it's ECONOMIC as compared to your ICE wheeler.Also, if you want to replace the rear tyre, you have to pay a premium price at the dealer, for regular tyre and fitting charges are 1200rs.This bike's sitting position, range, comfortable to ride, more like conventional bikes/mopeds, but it's not economic in any way, also definitely not eco-friendly, except that there's no smoke at running.Waste of money and inviting trouble after spending 40% more as compared with a comparable ICE 2 Wheeler.

review_text: In my previous review almost a year back - but to the company official - yes, it was a challenge to understand the product then from the sales guys, but I assume with a year gone it must have been smooth.Tvs iqube has the smoothest ride of all the available e-bikes on market - you may say the silence is the only negative - as we are still not accustomed to silent vehicles passing from behind - we still want the loud noise to understand a passing vehicle. Disclosure - I have tested almost all brands of ev vehicles - and I own 3 evs from different brands - iqube - ather - ampere.I use them for my delivery business and each one has crossed over 18,000 kmwithin a year of purchase - so I can proudly say that now my 1 vehicle is already free (saved on avg 450 ltrs of petrol from each bike - 1350 x 110 = 1,48,500)Buying from a well-established 2-wheeler company makes the most sense and on top of it I will rate tvs as number 1 compared to others - ride - handling - ease - ladies friendly - decent mileage - built quality etc.Tvs has one more issue of an onboard battery - which by default goes kaput around 15,000 kms- this is where we faced our first real problem with tvs - but they were able to sort it out in 15 days after repeated follow-ups. They also said that this onboard battery is no longer there in the newer vehicles. So assume this issue was for the initial bikes.Servicingandmaintenanceis an eye wash for all evs - as I really don't think there is anything that needs to be done like an engine vehicle - they just need to check the connectors and tighten the bolts.All pros and no cons (i wonder who gave 1-star ratings)

review_text: Model: TVS iQube 3.4 kWh (S variant)Usage: Daily office commute (approx40 km)​Sidhi baat karunga, bina kisi marketing jargon ke. Maine ye scooter 6 mahine pehle liya tha kyunki petrol ka kharcha control se bahar ho raha tha. Ola aur Ather bhi dekhe the, par family ke hisab se TVS thoda reliable laga aur seat comfortable thi.​Achhi Baatein (Pros):​Comfort Next Level Hai: Suspension bohot badhiya tune kiya hai. Shehar ke gaddhe (potholes) yeh aaram se nikal leti hai. Mere papa ko bhi back pain ki shikayat nahi hoti ispe.​Silent Ride: Start karke pata hi nahi chalta ki gaadi on hai. Bilkul silent aur smooth pickup hai. Eco mode mein city ride ke liye power sufficient hai.​Real Range: Company wale145 kmbolte hain, par sach ye hai ki Eco mode mein mujhe100-105 kmki range milti hai single rider ke saath. Power mode mein ye75-80 kmtak gir jati hai. But honestly, daily use ke liye100 kmbohot hai.​Jo Cheezein Kharab Lagi (Cons):​Software Glitches: Mobile app kabhi-kabhi connect nahi hota ya data late update karta hai. Screen thodi laggy feel hoti hai kabhi kabhi start karte waqt.​Glossy Plastic: Maine black colour liya tha, uspe scratches bohot jaldi dikhte hain. Agar lo toh matte finish dekhna ya lamination karwa lena day 1 pe.​Charging Time: 0 se 100 hone mein lagbhag 5 ghante lagte hain. Agar bhool gaye charge karna toh fast charging ka option nahi hai, jo kabhi kabhi dikkat deta hai.​ServiceExperience:Servicecenter wale thode slow hain. Firstservicemein unhone sirf wash kiya aur bolts tighten kiye, par time bohot lagaya. TVS ko EVservicepe thoda dhyaan dena padega.​Final Verdict:Agar aapko racing nahi karni aur ek "fill it, shut it, forget it" wali feeling chahiye family ke liye, toh iQube best hai. Ola S1 Pro bhagti tez hai par iQube chalti smooth hai. Maine ab tak4500 kmchalaya hai aur touchwood koi major breakdown nahi hua.​Rating: 4/5 (Ek star software aur charging time ke liye kata).​Pro Tip: Isko chalate waqt regeneration (brake lagane pe battery charge hona) ka dhyaan rakhna, range badh jayegi.

review_text: Buying the TVS iQube was the biggest mistake I've ever made. The scooter might look good, but themaintenanceand repair costs are unbelievably high. Even for minor issues, theservicecenter charges a huge amount - and half the time, they don't even fix the problem properly.Theservicecenters are the worstpart. The staff are untrained, careless, and never have the required sparepartsin stock. You'll end up visiting multiple times for the same issue, wasting both time and money. Customer support is useless - they just give excuses or tell you to "wait for updates."I'm extremely disappointed with TVS. If you're thinking about buying the iQube, don't! It's not worth the constant repairs, poorservice, and endless frustration.

review_text: After a year of using the TVS iQube S, I've faced constant issues that have made owning this scooter a nightmare. The battery started failing just a year in, when I took it to the Thrissurservicecentre in Kuriyichira, they kept the scooter for an entire month to sort out the issue. Shortly after getting it back, yet another problem arose. When I returned to theservicecentre, they assured me it was fixed, but just four days later, the scooter broke down again in the middle of the highway. I called theservicecentre for help, but they simply told me to bring it back myself, without offering any road assistance. This experience has highlighted the poor performance, excessiveservicedelays, and lack of reliable support for this scooter. I wouldn't recommend the TVS iQube S to anyone considering an electric scooter.

review_text: Maine TVS iQube 3.5 kWh variant liya hai aur overall experience kaafi achha raha hai. Buying experience smooth tha, showroom staff cooperative tha aur delivery time bhi reasonable tha. Scooter ka look premium aur simple hai, jo family aur daily office use dono ke liye perfect hai. Riding experience bahut comfortable hai, suspension city roads ke liye achha kaam karta hai aur braking bhi confident feel hoti hai.Performance ki baat karein to pickup smooth hai aur traffic me chalana easy hai. Real life me mujhe single charge me around150-155 kmka range mil jata hai, jo daily use ke liye sufficient hai. Charging ghar par easily ho jati hai aurmaintenancecost bahut kam hai, petrol scooter se kaafi sasta padta hai.

review_text: My experience has been nothing short of frustrating. Within just 1 year and 4 months, the accelerator jammed, leaving me stranded and forcing me to take the vehicle to the showroom at my own expense. That was just the beginning. A month later, the battery developed a problem, and now I've been without my two-wheeler for over three weeks. Battery life was just 1 1/2 years for this vehicle.Theservicehas been slow and unhelpful, and I'm deeply disappointed with the reliability of this vehicle. What's the point of investing in an electric scooter if it fails you so soon? I expected better from TVS, but unfortunately, I've been left with nothing but regret. Would not recommend it.

review_text: ek modern aur eco-friendly electric scooter hai jo aaj ke samay ke liye bahut hi useful vehicle maana jata hai. Yeh scooter khaas taur par un logon ke liye design kiya gaya hai jo pollution ko kam karna chahte hain aur petrol ke badhte daamon se bachna chahte hain. Iska design simple, stylish aur attractive hai, jo har age group ke logon ko pasand aata hai. TVS iQube me powerful electric motor di gayi hai jo smooth aur noiseless ride provide karti hai. Iski top speed lagbhag 78 km/h tak hoti hai, jo daily city commuting ke liye kaafi hai. Is scooter ki sabse badi khasiyat iska smart technology features hain, jaise ki digital display, smartphone connectivity aur navigation support. Isme lithium-ion battery use hoti hai jo ek baar full charge hone par lagbhag 100 km tak chal sakti hai, jo roz ke kaam ke liye perfect range hai. Charging bhi kaafi convenient hai kyunki ise ghar par hi easily charge kiya ja sakta hai. Safety ke liye isme regenerative braking system, strong body aur LED light.

review_text: I recently moved to busy city for work and I realized I needed vehicle that was compact, easy to handle and hassle-free in traffic. I wanted something eco friendly and low maintenance as I also care about reducing my carbon footprint. After checking out various options I decided to buy TVS iQube. I mostly use it to commute to work and run quick errands around town. The scooter is comfortable and the under seat storage is handy for carrying essentials like laptop bag. Charging at home is convenient and I rarely worry about running out of battery within the city limits. Overall it has made daily rides stress free and enjoyable. I would highly recommend TVS iQube for anyone looking for reliable, modern and eco friendly scooter. It is ideal for city rides and daily errands but maybe not the best choice for long highway trips due to battery range limitations. For anyone wanting stylish, convenient and environmentally friendly ride the iQube is perfect option.

review_text: My younger brother recently moved to the city and struggled with crowded roads on his old scooter. While helping him I saw the iQube and was curious. After talking to the dealer and riding it myself I realized it offered smooth handling without noise or vibration. Its modern look and electric efficiency made it an easy choice for our hectic urban commutes. The scooter has been surprisingly versatile. Maneuvering through the narrow lanes or busy intersections feels effortless. I even experimented with short ride to the riverside in evening and bike performed flawlessly. It is quiet, responsive and surprisingly agile for an electric vehicle making every trip enjoyable. Definitely. After seeing how smoothly the iQube handled the city chaos I couldn't stop thinking about my friends and relatives struggling on older scooters. I told my younger brother's colleague to try it out and watching him glide effortlessly through tight lanes convinced me even more it's perfect for anyone who wants a hassle free, reliable ride. Every time I see the scooter parked outside I remember how much easier it made our daily errands, and I know anyone who rides it will feel the same sense of relief and satisfaction.

review_text: One evening on my way home my old scooter stalled again in the middle of traffic. I was so fed up that I walked it to the side and watched a guy glide past silently on an iQube. Out of curiosity I asked him about it and he let me take a short ride. The smooth pickup and quiet feel instantly hit me I knew this was the upgrade I needed. My daily rides changed completely. The scooter moves gently but confidently through tight gaps and the silence makes the whole trip feel calmer. Even rough patches near my lane do not bother me much now. The display keeps me aware of range and routes and the battery easily lasts through my entire day. Charging at night has become a simple habit. I have already told few friends they should try it because it genuinely makes city riding easier. The balance feels reassuring even for someone not used to EVs. You save on fuel, you avoid engine noise and you get a smooth, predictable ride every day.

review_text: I first noticed the iQube outside a shop where I usually stop before work. A delivery rider parked it next to me and the design instantly caught my eye. When he told me he had ridden nearly 40 km already without a drop in performance I got curious. A week later I walked into a showroom just to check and ended up taking a long test ride that completely surprised me. The moment I began using it daily I felt how different an electric ride can be. The acceleration feels clean, almost like it is floating forward. Even during peak hour chaos the scooter remains steady and predictable. The seat is comfortable enough for long commutes and I barely feel tired after office trips. The calm hum while riding makes city noise feel less aggressive. I recommend it because it has genuinely made my routine easier and more peaceful. Many people deals with the traffic jams or long city loops will appreciate how light and effortless it feels. Charging it does not disturb your schedule and the battery performance stays consistent. For people wanting a modern, dependable EV with comfort, the iQube is genuinely worth considering.

review_text: I decided to try the iQube after my cousin visited from Bangalore and arrived riding his new electric scooter. He talked about how much money he saved each month and insisted I take a spin. The moment I glided down the lane I felt something I had never felt on any of my previous rides complete smoothness without any engine noise. That one short loop convinced me instantly. My experience since then has been surprisingly pleasant. The throttle responds quickly but not aggressively, perfect for busy roads near my home. The scooter feels well balanced even when carrying groceries or my laptop bag. The screen gives clear info and I have never felt anxious about range. Even during rainy evenings, the grip and stability have never let me down. I'd suggest this scooter to anyone who wants calmer, cleaner and more predictable way to travel. It saves time, cuts fuel expenses and gives a comfortable ride every single day. Friends who try it are always impressed by how natural it feels. If you are thinking about switching to an EV without complications, this one delivers exactly what you need.

But those who prefer petrol bikes for speed, power, or long rides may not be satisfied.

review_text: The TVS iQube is one of the most practical and well-built electric scooters in India right now. Designed for city riders who want a balance of efficiency, comfort, and technology, it delivers a truly modern riding experience. Smooth & Silent Ride: The motor is powerful enough for quick city commutes, yet completely silent. The instant torque makes traffic riding effortless. Good Range Options: Depending on the variant, you can comfortably expect 80-150 km of real-world range, which is more than enough for daily use. Solid Build Quality: The fit and finish feel premium. Suspension and ride quality are excellent, even on rough city roads. Smart Features: TFT display, app connectivity, riding modes, reverse assist, and regenerative braking make it stand out. Low Running Cost: Charging at home is cheap and convenient, with very little maintenance compared to petrol scooters. Charging takes a few hours (overnight is best), and fast-charging options are limited. Higher-battery variants cost more, though they offer extra range. Not ideal for very long highway trips-this scooter shines in the city The TVS iQube is perfect for riders who want an eco-friendly, stylish, and reliable electric scooter without sacrificing comfort. It combines modern features, strong performance, and trusted TVS reliability. If your daily commute is within the city and you have access to charging at home, this scooter is one of the best EV scooter

review_text: Best electric vehicle with feature and safety The TVS iQube is a commendable step by the Indian two-wheeler giant into the electric mobility space. With its clean, no-nonsense design, the iQube appeals to both young professionals and family riders who want a balance between modern technology and practical commuting. Powered by a 4.4 kW electric motor, the iQube delivers a smooth and quiet ride with decent acceleration, especially in urban traffic conditions. Its top speed of around 78 km/h and a real-world range of 100-110 km make it suitable for daily city commutes. The scooter supports fast charging, taking around 4-5 hours to reach full charge on a standard charger which is acceptable for overnight

review_text: Review for TVS iQube Variant: PLEASE DONT BUY The TVS iQube ST variant is a highly unsafe and unreliable vehicle and DANGEROUS. Despite being just 3 months old with 2,000 km driven, it has a critical issue where the vehicle shuts off suddenly while in motion, posing a major risk to life. I've faced this problem multiple times, even resulting in an accident when a vehicle hit me from behind after my scooter abruptly turned off. I've provided TVS with video, images, and CCTV footage as proof. TVS has had my vehicle for testing several times, claiming software updates would fix the issue, but the problem persists. They've acknowledged similar complaints from other customers but have no solution. Other issues include frequent OTP glitches, incorrect date/time settings, overheating of the motor, and strange noises when braking. Despite repeated escalations and requests for a refund or replacement, TVS has not taken any responsibility. Dealers deflect the issue, saying it's the manufacturer's problem. This vehicle is a serious safety hazard. I strongly advise against buying the TVS iQube. If you'd like to discuss or see the evidence, you can reach me at Roshan - 9845275081. Thanks & Regards, Roshan

review_text: I bought the TVS iQube a few months ago, and so far, it has been nothing but a delightful experience. The buying process was smooth where the dealership staff guided perfectly about subsidies and financing available. Quite remarkable riding comfort when silent and smooth, particularly suited to city commutes. But it's slightly bland unless one is crazy about white. Performance: the 2.2kWh is quite decent but doesn't give much power to keep going. Yet it serves its purpose since acceleration into urban traffic and so would be ideal; dashboard design could've improved on smart with some fancy navigation. Maintenance has been pretty low, and the process of servicing was hassle-free. Overall, the iQube combines practicality with eco-friendliness and a pinch of tech. Highly recommended for those considering an electric two-wheeler! Rating: 4.2/5

review_text: I bought the TVS iQube just a few months after riding it, and I must say that I'm immensely impressed. It runs silently, gives a very smooth ride, and the plugging-in convenience at home overnight in my apartment so that you wake up to a fully charged battery has been amazing. The range might not be as exciting if you have longer trips, but for a daily commute it's perfect. Of course, the iQube has definitely changed my outlook on transportation. It's not just a piece of transport; it is a statement to a greener future.

review_text: It has been a revitalizing experience to ride the TVS iQube, and as an electric scooter fanatic, I have to say it meets up to expectations. Its performance similarly amazed me, as did its clean, futuristic style. Priced in India at about 1.15 lakh, it's a fantastic way to get started with electric mobility without going broke.My everyday trips are ideal for the iQube's about 75 km range on a full charge. Smooth acceleration and easy handling in city traffic characterize the scooter. The smooth, quiet ride-no more engine noise is the nicest aspect. I now charge it over night as part of my routine, and I really like how affordable and practical it is in comparison to bikes that run on petrol. Modern touches like the linked app, geofencing, and digital dashboard improve riding overall. It's been nice to switch to electric and I feel like I'm helping to make the world a cleaner place when I ride the TVS iQube.

review_text: The TVS iQube is a sleek and stylish electric scooter that delivers a smooth and efficient ride for urban commuters. With its futuristic design and advanced features, it's a standout in the electric scooter market. Performance-wise, the iQube impresses with its zippy acceleration and nimble handling, making it perfect for navigating city streets. The electric motor delivers ample power, ensuring a quick and responsive ride, while the regenerative braking system adds to its efficiency and range. One of the standout features of the iQube is its smart connectivity options. With the dedicated mobile app, riders can remotely monitor their scooter's battery status, track their ride history, and even locate their scooter in a crowded parking lot. This level of connectivity adds convenience and peace of mind for users. In terms of range, the iQube offers a respectable distance on a single charge, making it suitable for daily commuting needs. While it may not have the longest range in its class, it strikes a good balance between range and performance. Build quality is another area where the iQube shines. TVS has crafted a well-built scooter with attention to detail, ensuring durability and longevity. The materials used feel premium, and the overall fit and finish are impressive. However, there are a few areas where the iQube could improve. The charging infrastructure for electric vehicles is still developing in many regions, which could pose a challenge for users who don't have easy access to charging stations. Additionally, the price point of the iQube may be a deterrent for some potential buyers, as it sits at a slightly higher price compared to its petrol-powered counterparts. Overall, the TVS iQube is a standout electric scooter that offers a winning combination of performance, style, and connectivity. It's a great option for urban commuters looking to make the switch to electric mobility.

review_text: I recently bought an Ather Rizta Mid variant with a pro pack (pro pack is essential for the 5-year (additional 2 years from original 3 years) warranty they claim on ads and costs you close to 20k). I have also bought an additional 3 years battery warranty for 5k.Let me talk about the deliveryexperience, I have purchased the vehicle through a local dealer (rajdurai's Coimbatore, Ramnagar). The initialexperiencewas good until I paid the whole money, till then the sales guy was behind me every day ringing at least 5-6 times asking if I can make it the next day. The moment I paid I stopped getting calls from the sales guy, in fact, the guy was missing during delivery and I (myself and my family) had to wait for an 1 hour without any updates, not sure about if this happens in other showrooms. Sales guys show a lot of attitude is what I understand from the reviews on Google for the showroom, Ather should fix this to sustain in the market, they should understand that they are not a monopoly anymore, there are several electric bikes in the market with 8 years default warranty with better configurations. I also hear patheticexperiencesabout the service especially on the Coimbatore, Ramnagar branch. I am already worried about my wrong purchase decision, hope not to get any issues in between to avoid the pathetic service team and the hassle.

review_text: The Best Electric Companion for Families: Ather Rizta Review The Ather Rizta stands out as a perfect electric scooter designed with families in mind. With a powerful 4.3 kW Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor, it delivers a top speed of 80 km/h, balancingperformanceand safety for everydayridingneeds. The scooter offers an impressive range of up to 123 km on a full charge, making it ideal for daily urban commuting without frequent charging breaks. Comfort features include the longest seat segment in its class, accommodating riders and pillions comfortably, along with a spacious under-seat storage capacity of 34 liters, enough to fit a full-face helmet. The scooter is equipped with advanced safety tech like Ather Skid Control, preventing wheel slip on slippery surfaces, enhancing rider confidence and security. It also features digital instrumentation, Bluetooth connectivity, navigation, mobile app integration, and a quick charging time of around 8 hours

review_text: Myexperiencewith my EV has been nothing short of fantastic. One of the things I appreciate most is the convenience of charging. During the recent monsoon season, I was a bit worried about how it would perform. To my surprise, it handled the heavy rain and slick roads with no problem at all. The EV was reliable and performed exceptionally well, which has given me great peace of mind.This vehicle is more than just a mode of transportation; it's a joy todrive. Its impressive suspension system and reliableperformancein various weather conditions make it a standout choice. Whether I'm charging up at home, cruising with friends, or navigating through a rainstorm, it consistently exceeds my expectations. I can confidently say that this EV has been a fantastic investment.Alternative VersionsHere are a couple of other ways you could phrase your review, depending on the tone you're going for.Option 1: More Enthusiastic"I am absolutely in love with my electric vehicle! I've had the car with three people in it and it handles the weight and bumps effortlessly, providing a consistently smooth and comfortableridethat never fails to impress.And it's not just a fair-weather vehicle. I've driven it through heavy monsoon downpours, and it performed flawlessly. There was no loss of traction or any issues, which speaks volumes about its reliability. This EV has proven itself time and time again, whether it's the ease of charging, the exceptional comfort with a full load, or its robustperformancein the rain. It's a vehicle that brings a smile to my face every time I get behind the wheel."Option 2: More Concise and Direct"My EV has exceeded all my expectations. The ease of charging is a major highlight, making ownership a breeze. Therideis exceptionally smooth thanks to the outstanding suspension. It handles a full load of three people without any dip inperformanceor comfort, which is truly impressive. I've also tested its limits during the monsoon season, and it has not disappointed. It's a reliable and resilient vehicle that performs perfectly in all conditions."

review_text: I bought Ather Rizta from MST Motors, Aizawl, on 9th September 2024 and have ridden around 3600 km in six months. I had been wanting an Electric Scooter for a while, but the Ather 450 felt too small. So, I waited for Rizta, which is bigger and more family friendly. As soon as it hits the market, I booked it in advance, and it arrived after a month. The staff at MST Motors were great, they handled everything smoothly. Regarding the riding experience. Riza is smooth, agile and very easy to handle. I love the speed and comfort. But, the passenger foot pegs are too small, making it uncomfortable for the passenger. I don't know why they made it this way. And yes, the pickup could be better. One cool feature is the Magic twist where you can twist the throttle forward, which is in opposite to normal acceleration, to slow down, acting like an engine braking system while also charging the battery. It's very useful on mild slopes and while cornering, but on steep slopes, it makes the scooter skid, so better avoid using in those situations. Pros & Cons: I. Pros: 1) Fast and Smooth handling. 2) Easy to ride. 3) Good storage space. 4) Charges while going downhill. 5) Magic twist acts as an engine brake and recharges the battery. II. Cons: 1) Passenger footpegs are too small. 2) No main stand, so parking on slopes is tricky and difficult. 3) No brake lock, making uphill and downhill parking difficult (I often use a rubber band to tie the rear brake lever). 4) Eco Mode is inconsistent, sometimes strong, sometimes weak. 5) Magic twist can cause skidding on steep slopes. 6) Can overheat if riding uphill for too long. I really enjoy riding the Rizta. And it consumes about 1% battery per 1 KM, but Since Aizawl is full of hills, mileage varies. The pickup is smooth as cutting butter, but on long steep uphill roads, it can overheat. If that happens, you'll need to wait around 1 minute for it to cool down. It handles .potholes way better than Suzuki Access and Honda Dio, which we also own. However, parking on slopes is a big headache. On the bright side, Ather's after-sales service is excellent, and customer support is top notch. If Ather improves parking issues and passenger comfort on Rizta, especially the footpegs and brake lock, Rizta could be an even better choice for hilly areas.

review_text: The buying experience seems good, i waited for appx. 13 days for its delivery. Let me explain this review in2 parts, the pros and cons.PROS1) The ride quality is smooth like a butter, and seating comfort is upright, weight is little heavy and so vey smooth and stable ride even at cruising 80s and 90s.2) The performance is like a beast, having 3 modes (eco, normal and sport) With sudden pickup, it can easily go 80s and 90s within some seconds. Truly a beast. Can go 43kmph on eco, 82 on normal and 115 on sport3) Range seems good. I really get the range displaying on screen with slightly 1 or2 kmfluctuating. With steady speed, I get 130+ in normal (25 percent discharge on riding35 kms),164kmin eco mode (11 % dropped on riding18kms), haven't tested sport mode. I feel satisfied with the range. Note: KEEP THE BRAKE BY WIRE AND REGEN TO HIGH TO GET THIS RANGE4) Other notable pros are, headlight is extremely powerful making the road very clear. The boot is very large of over 34 litres which is very useful. The cruise control and reverse is very useful. The suspension seems smooth, previously riden activa, there the suspension is worst, but this ola handles speedbrakers and holes well. There is an app called ola, which shows the battery %,servicerelated tickets, etc. is goodTHE CONS1) We all know the most important con in ola - THESERVICEyes it's the worstpartin ola. My charger was repaired within a week. I took it to theservicecentre. They agreed to replace it. They told me to wait for 1 week but my new charger is getting so much delayed and finally, after raising multiple mails and all, got the charger after 26 days.Other thanserviceissues, I really like this machine. Finally 4 stars for the vehicle and 1 star for theservice.Ola is really great at making machines, and good at innovatoion you know when you ride on it. But Ola is inconsistent inservice(which is the backbone of vehicles), which is the worstpart.

review_text: I have been riding the OLA S1 X for the last few months, and overall my experience has been very good. The pickup is smooth, and the scooter feels powerful while riding in city traffic. The acceleration is impressive, and it becomes easy to overtake vehicles on busy roads. The design also looks modern and premium which attracts attention on the road.The battery performance is decent for daily office travel and local rides. I usually get good range in eco mode and charging is also convenient at home. The riding comfort is excellent because the seat is wide and suspension handles rough roads properly. The digital display and smart features make the scooter feel advanced compared to traditional petrol scooters.Maintenancecost is very low and that is one of the biggest advantages. The scooter is silent and easy to handle even in crowded areas. Braking performance is also confidence-inspiring. The boot space is enough for carrying daily essentials and helmet.There are still some areas where improvement is needed like software response andservicesupport in some locations, but overall the experience has been positive. For daily commuting and city rides, OLA S1 X is a good electric scooter with stylish looks, strong performance and low running cost.Pros:Strong pickup and smooth rideLow running andmaintenancecostStylish modern designComfortable seatingGood smart featuresCons:Serviceexperience can improveMinor software lag sometimesReal-world range depends on riding mode.

review_text: I didn't want an overpriced EV, so I looked for something that balanced price and features. After visiting a few showrooms and watching real user reviews, the Ola S1 X fit my needs perfectly. It offered good range and a clean look. I ride my Ola S1 X every day, and it's very comfortable even on uneven roads. The scooter handles traffic easily, and the seat feels supportive during longer trips. I feel in control at all times. I use the Ola S1 X daily to go to work, and the ride is surprisingly smooth. Even on uneven streets, it doesn't feel shaky. Sitting for half an hour doesn't hurt, which is rare for electric scooters. I'd tell my friends to try it.

It felt modern and practical, and that's what made me buy it. The Ola S1 X goes a long way on a single charge. My usual city commute feels worry-free since I do not have to charge often. It makes my commute faster and less tiring. Definitely worth recommending to anyone in the city.

review_text: I bought the Ola S1 X mainly for my everyday city rides. I wanted something easy to handle and pocket-friendly. After checking a few options, I liked the range and comfort this scooter offered. Buying it was quick and the process was simple. Riding the Ola S1 X through the city during my daily commute is quite comfortable. The seat is soft, and the scooter is able to handle the traffic in a very effective way. When picking around small potholes, it also remains stable, and during long rides, I do not feel fatigue. The screen and controls on the Ola S1 X are easy to figure out. I didn't have to read manuals for hours. Riding felt convenient, and the lights and signals are clear. Definitely something I'd recommend for anyone wanting a practical e-scooter.

The vehicle, once it's out of the showroom, has ongoing problems that to date they haven't identified or fix.The sales team manipulates, management stays silent, and no one takes ownership after sales.Battery replacement after warranty costs a shocking ₹90k - pure scam. review_text: It's a classic case of expectation versus reality when it comes to EV range. While the127 kmIDC (Indian Drive Cycle) figure looks great on the brochure, it's recorded under "ideal" laboratory conditions that rarely account for the chaos of daily traffic.In my experience over the last few days, if you're navigating stop-and-go signals or carrying a pillion, that100 kmmark is the absolute ceiling. The moment you get impatient and push the speedo past 50 or 60 kmph, you can practically watch the battery percentage drop in real-time. It seems the Chetak is happiest cruising at a leisurely pace in Eco mode; treat it like a performance bike, and you'll be looking for a charging point much sooner than anticipated. For anyone considering it, definitely factor in a 20-25% buffer from the official claims to avoid range anxiety during your commute.

review_text: - I purchased the Chetak e-bike, but it has been highly unreliable since day one.- The bike has stopped multiple times in the middle of the road, creating unsafe situations.- It frequently experiences sudden jerks or hiccups while riding.- Despite its high price, the warranty coverage on key components (battery, motor, lock, etc.) is very limited.- Theservicecenters provide extremely poor support and are difficult to deal with.- I chose Chetak because of its well-known brand name, but the experience has been disappointing.- In terms of reliability and practicality, it performs no better - or even worse - than many other e-bikes.- An electric bike like this isn't practical or safe for daily commuting, as it can suddenly break down without warning.- Within just this year, I have visited theservicecenter more than six times.- On two occasions, the bike broke down completely and had to be towed to theservicecenter.- Based on my experience, a petrol bike would be a much more dependable option.

review_text: I have been facing a triangle error since purchasing the scooter. Initially, the error occurred once a month, but the frequency has gradually increased. I took the scooter to theservicecentre 2-3 times for this issue, and the issue was resolved temporarily. As I drive the scooter daily, I get this error again and again. The next time I took the scooter to theservicecentre, they replaced the accelerator pad within warranty, but after 5 days the same issue occurred. My main concern is that the vehicle speed immediately slows down to 20 kph while driving on the highway at a consistent speed of 50-60 kph. I think this issue can cause a major accident if not properly resolved. I have already raised a complaint, but as usual, my problem has been temporarily resolved, and no one can guarantee the problem will not occur again.

review_text: Bajaj Chetak claims up to120 kmrange on a full charge, but in real-world usage, it delivers much less. Since regularly draining the battery to 0% is not practical or recommended, charging is usually needed when the battery reaches around 25-30%. As a result, the usable range is only about70 kmper charge, which is significantly lower than the advertised figure.

review_text: Design & Build Quality:The Chetak retains the classic scooter silhouette with a premium, retro-modern design. The metal body panels feel solid, and the fit-and-finish is among the best in its segment. The LED lighting, horseshoe-shaped DRL, and neatly integrated digital console give it an upmarket feel.Performance & Ride Quality:It comes with a 4.0 kW (peak) electric motor offering two riding modes - Eco and Sport. In Sport mode it feels peppy enough for city commuting, while Eco mode stretches range. Acceleration is smooth and silent; top speed hovers around 63-70 km/h depending on variant. Suspension is tuned for comfort, easily absorbing typical Indian road undulations.Battery & Range:The 3 kWh lithium-ion battery is IP67-rated for water and dust resistance. Real-world range is about 85-95 kmper charge in Eco mode, slightly less in Sport. Charging from a standard home socket takes roughly 4-5 hours. The battery is non-removable, which means home charging is easiest if you have a parking space with a power point.

review_text: The Bajaj Chetak seamlessly blends classic styling with modern electric tech. It prioritises robust build quality and smooth city riding over aggressive performance. It is one of the best premium electric scooters for daily commuting.What Makes It Great: Exceptional Build Quality: Unlike competitors using plastic bodies, the Chetak features a solid metal body. It feels sturdy, premium, and built to handle rough roads. Smooth Power Delivery: Acceleration is predictable and linear. It mimics an automatic petrol scooter, making it incredibly easy for beginners to ride. Predictable Real-World Range: The digital range indicator is highly accurate. You can confidently get 90-100 kmon a single charge in Eco mode. Premium Features: The vibrant colour display, turn-by-turn navigation, and sturdy under-seat storage add massive daily utility. ReliableServiceNetwork: Backed by Bajaj's massive legacy network, finding sparepartsand getting reliableservicingis hassle-free.

review_text: I recently bought the Bajaj Chetak, and my overall experience has been very good. The scooter looks premium and stylish, especially with the metal body design. Riding quality is smooth and comfortable for daily city use. The pickup is decent and the scooter feels stable even on rough roads. Battery performance is also good and I am getting satisfactory range on a single charge.The buying experience at the showroom was easy and staff members were helpful.Maintenancecost is low compared to petrol scooters and charging at home is convenient. The digital display and smart features make the scooter modern and user-friendly.Pros: Good mileage/range, premium design, low running cost, smooth riding experience.Cons: Charging time could be slightly faster and storage space is average.Overall, Bajaj Chetak is a great electric scooter for daily commuting and I would definitely recommend it to others.

review_text: It is the only EV scooter which has direct drive motor, which ensures smooth power delivery prevents Power loss. Battery is placed in foot area by which the user gets huge boot space, App connectivity which provides Geo fencing, Remote immobilization, Real time monitoring, Over speeding alert and also Speed lock. Suspension could have been improved, since it only features single-sided leading link suspension in the front and an offset mono-shock at the rear It feels rough especially in hilly potholes and unexpected bumps, Center stand often messes with the edges of the roads in hills I recommend Chetak for its torque in hills especially when compared to IQube and Ather both feature Belt drive system which requires more maintenance in Hills. In a Slope Belt drive system is very laggy while a still start situation it is crucial. Chetak offers a smooth power delivery in hills.

review_text: I bought Bajaj Chetak 3501 being influenced by the tag line 'ye to Shola Hai ' and enjoyed riding it from New year 2025 , but there is a recurrent problem which the Company must fix for ensuring customer satisfaction. The issue is related to Auxiliary battery which becomes fully discharged and the Vehicle becomes immobile , even the unlocking of the handle is impossible. As a result the vehicle is towed to the Service Centre. I had already faced it twice in the past four months and now the vehicle is immobile for third time. The Service personnel are quite responsive in fixing it within an hour, but towing the vehicle is always a big hassle and it really questions the versatility of 3501. I wish I should have waited for four months more to get a Chetak 3503. Shall be thankful if Bajaj fixes this problem in 3501 by recalling this model , because I feel the owners of Chetak 3501 must be facing the similar problems in their vehicles too. Thanks

review_text: The Worst Scooter I've Ever Purchased - Bajaj Chetak is a Disaster! After 4 months of owning the Bajaj Chetak and spending almost a month in the workshop for endless issues, I can confidently say-this is the worst scooter I've ever bought. Despite being a loyal Bajaj fan with a Pulsar and two Avengers in my garage, the Chetak has completely shattered my trust in the brand. Pathetic Sales Experience If you think walking into Khivraj Bajaj to buy a Chetak means you'll get treated like a customer-think again. The sales team is absolutely useless. They don't care about selling the scooter, let alone assisting customers. You'll have to beg them to take your money and do the paperwork. It's as if they're doing you a favor by selling their own product! Service? More Like a Nightmare! If you think the bad experience ends after purchase, the real torture begins with service. Every time you take the scooter in for repairs, they act like they'll fix it, but in reality, they do NOTHING.

They don’t care about selling the scooter, let alone assisting customers. You’ll have to beg them to take your money and do the paperwork. It’s as if they’re doing you a favor by selling their own product! Service? More Like a Nightmare! If you think the bad experience ends after purchase, the real torture begins with service. Every time you take the scooter in for repairs, they act like they’ll fix it, but in reality, they do NOTHING.

review_text: I bought Hero Vida V2 in March 25 and have driven it for 800 kms so far. Things I like about the scooter are: 1. Design: It looks like a premium scooter with well balanced chasis. The key provided has some good features like key to switch the headlight, sound alerts etc. 2. Service centres: The hero service centres are available almost every where so minor issues are handled quite fast. 3. Removable battery: My scooter charger cable was damaged by rat so I couldn't charge it directly. Thankfully, I could charge the battery separate after which I got the cable replaced. It is very under rated feature and is literally a life saver in case of electronic issues. Cons: 1. Range: Even with two batteries, range is only 100 km/charge. Hero claims 145 km/charge which is totally wrong. 2. Mobile app: Most of features never work in mobile app. The software update is also almost negligent. 3. Seat: Split seat is weirdly designed and feels uncomfortable for big rider. Space under seat is also quite low.

review_text: Not everyone will be inclined to read a lengthy review, so here is a summaryDO NOT BUY, DO NOT BUYPOORLY ENGINEERED PRODUCTHORRIBLE AFTER SALES SERVICEMOBILE APP IS A JOKEYOU HAVE BEEN WARNEDRead on and decide if you want to go through what I have described below.I have owned a Vida plus for over a year now. Let's get done with the pros since that's a short list - Smooth, Surprisingly good suspension, decent looks. Overall, Hero has dropped the ball in this one. Mine has been in the workshop for a total of 45+ days over time just for fixing glitches. The cruise control did not work and the mobile app did not connect on Day-1. The dealership did not have the 'machine' to debug so they couldn't help. Even after they received the device, they could not determine why the cruise control was not engaging. They eventually did after keeping my scooter for about 15 days and consulting another technician at a different dealership but only after I escalated with Hero. Next in line was the auxiliary battery. The scooter would work but display would be blank. Next day, the scooter refused to start. Called the RSA and had it delivered to the dealership. They simply recharged the auxiliary battery and gave it back. This happened 2 times within a month. When I questioned if they looked into why the battery keeps draining and if it needs replacement, I was pointed to a printout that had addresses of Battery dealers (20 kms away from me) and asked to contact them. Ideally, it's the dealership's job to coordinate battery replacement. Same issue happened within a couple of weeks. I think the vehicle was sold to me with a defective battery. Frustrated, I paid for a battery replacement and the vehicle was working OK for some time. Unfortunately, I lost the keyfob one day. Contacted the dealership and was quoted 3500. I agreed and went to the workshop only to be told that 'The software is being updated. We'll call you when done'. It's been a week and I haven't received that call yet. Now comes the Vida App. Absolute piece of crap. It's supposed to work as a keyfob. Honestly, you have a better chance at winning a lottery than starting the vehicle using the App. I have literally spent 25 minutes just today to get the scooter to start using the app. I guess while the dealership is working on the 'Software Upgrade ', my scooter has become practically usable. Overall, a very poor ownershipexperience. I am going to sell my scooter once I get a new keyfob.

review_text: Hero Vida V1 - Not Worth the Hype⭐ Rating: 1/5📅 Reviewed on: 22 June 2025📍 Location: Alathur, Kerala🛠️ Dealer: Ayaana Motors🛵 Variant: Hero Vida V1 PlusPros:Decent designSmoothride(when it works)Cons:Pathetic batteryperformanceThe start button is not working when it comes to rainFast charging is not supported even in the listed place of the app.There is a mobile charging port. But no place to keep the mobileRude dealershipexperienceZero accountability from Hero MotocorpNo support, no solution, no empathyDetailed Review:I had high expectations when I bought the Hero Vida V1. I was promised a range of 100 km. In reality, I barely get 60-70 km on a full charge-even in eco mode with minimal load, I am the only single person rider.I visited Ayaana Motors, Alathur, seeking help. I was shocked when the dealer owner himself told me "We can't give you an explanation. If you want, go to court."Is this how you treat a customer who invested hard-earned money in a brand-new EV?I even requested them to test my vehicle - take a 90 kmrideon full charge. They refused. Eventually, the owner agreed to "fix the battery", but refused sales return or any compensation.This leads to one sad conclusion:Either I was sold an old or faulty battery, or Hero is cheating customers with fake range claims.Would you buy a vehicle that could stop anytime, anywhere?Unless you're ready to tow a backup auto with you wherever you go, stay away from Hero Vida.Verdict:Hero's EV dream is turning into a nightmare for honest buyers. I regret my decision and warn others - think twice before trusting Hero Vida.

review_text: Having ridden your TVS iQube 3.5 kWh for three months and under5000 km, you find its comfort levels highly comparable to traditional petrol scooters. The145 kmIDC range variant offers a familiar, stable ride ideal for former ICE users. While you value features like vehicle location and navigation, the current software has significant friction points: the "current ride" data fails to reset automatically, making trip management difficult, and there is a strong preference for standard Google Maps over the proprietary app interface.

review_text: An awesome EV scooter packed with style, performance, build quality, and a lot of features offered by Hero at a reasonable price, especially when compared to other competitors in the market.

review_text: Vida V2 Pro ek electric scooter hai jo Hero MotoCorp ki taraf se launch kiya gaya hai. Ye scooter un logon ke liye ek achha option hai jo modern design, advanced features aur long range wali EV ki talash kar rahe hain. Iska design sleek aur stylish hai, jo urban youth ko attract karta hai. Build quality mazboot hai aur scooter par premium finishing di gayi hai.Battery aurperformanceki baat karein toh Vida V2 Pro me removable lithium-ion battery milti hai, jise ghar ya office dono jagah easily charge kiya ja sakta hai. Company ke claim ke hisaab se iski range 110-120 km ke aas-paas hai, jo daily cityrideske liye kaafi hai. Top speed lagbhag 80 kmph tak jati hai, jo city traffic ke liye sufficient hai. Acceleration bhi smooth hai aurridingexperiencecomfortable lagta hai.Features ke mamle me ye scooter advanced technology ke sath aata hai. Isme 7-inch TFT touch display diya gaya hai jisme navigation, call/SMS alerts, aurridingmodes jaise options milte hain. Cruise control, reverse assist aur customizableridingmodes jaise features ise apne segment me alag banate hain. Connectivity ke liye mobile app support bhi diya gaya hai jisse user scooter ki health, charging status aur location track kar sakta hai.Suspension aur braking system bhi balance aur reliable hai. Front me disc brake aur rear me drum brake diye gaye hain, jo safety ke point of view se effective hai. Comfort ke liye scooter me spacious seat aur achhi storage space bhi milti hai.Overall, Vida V2 Pro ek premium electric scooter hai jo stylish design, high-tech features aur reliableperformanceke sath aata hai. Jo log eco-friendly aur modern scooter chahte hain unke liye ye ek strong choice ho sakta hai.
